generated_comment: Arre, isme uska kya kasoor? Aadmi toh thoda emotionally weak ho jaata hai, but this woman knew he was married. Uski kya zaroorat thi phasaneki? These women only look for such chances, they just want to break families. Bechare ki family barbaad ho gayi uski wajah se.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment blames the 'other woman' entirely for an affair, excusing the man's responsibility ('aadmi toh thoda emotionally weak ho jaata hai'). It uses the 'she knew he was married' framing and portrays the woman as a 'homewrecker' ('break families', 'barbaad ho gayi uski wajah se') and a 'seductress' ('phasaneki', 'look for such chances').
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 39. Married. An upper caste Hindu man in Bhopal. You are sitting with four male colleagues in the office canteen during lunch. One colleague is telling the group that his 25 year old daughter told him she is in a live-in relationship with her boyfriend. You are one of four men reacting to this news. You are giving your opinion on live-in relationships and what a father's responsibility is when his daughter makes choices like this and what it says about how she was raised.
